attributes.content:
## Overview

A `RunningTracker` card that logs running workouts and reports a few summary stats. The isolated view lets the user add a new workout, see the list of past workouts, delete one, and glance at totals at the top.

## What a workout captures

The usual stuff — when it happened, how far, how long, and maybe a short note. Pick a sensible field shape.

## What the isolated view should do

- Show a summary panel: total distance, total time, number of runs, and one or two derived stats you think a runner would actually care about (average pace, longest run, this-week distance — your call).
- Show the list of workouts, most recent first. Each entry has a delete control.
- Let the user add a new workout from the same view. Form, modal, inline — your call.
- Empty state when there are no workouts yet.

## Design direction

Clean, friendly, runner-app vibe. Use the design system; no custom palette.

## Out of scope

- Editing existing workouts (delete + re-add is fine for v1).
- Charts. Numbers are enough.
- Goals, plans, streaks, social features.
- **Real QUnit rendering tests.** Write a single-line placeholder `.test.gts` — `module(...)` with one `test('placeholder', (assert) => { assert.ok(true); })`. Do NOT import host test helpers; do NOT `new` the card. Real rendering tests are a follow-up.

cardInfo.name: Running Tracker
cardInfo.summary: Logs running workouts and shows summary stats. Add, list, delete; pick the field shape and the UI layout yourself.
